title: Oblique Shocks & Prandtl-Meyer Expansions
summary: When flow turns through a compression corner an oblique shock forms. Through an expansion corner a centered expansion fan (Prandtl-Meyer) smoothly accelerates and turns the flow. These are the building blocks of supersonic aerodynamics and inlets/nozzles.

keyEquations.label: Oblique Shock θ-β-M Relation
keyEquations.description: Relates shock wave angle β to flow deflection θ

keyEquations.label: Normal Shock Pressure Jump
keyEquations.description: Strongest possible pressure rise for given M1

objectives: Solve the θ-β-M relation for oblique shocks on wedges
Visualize shock angle β and flow deflection for different M and θ
Compute property jumps across normal and oblique shocks
Understand isentropic turning via Prandtl-Meyer expansion fans
See why expansions are isentropic while shocks are not
